category_title: Dominant students with high levels of physical and verbal aggression
minimal_context: The student is working in a collaborative science group looking at photos to discuss animal needs and emotions.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please look at the photos of the animals with your group and discuss what you think they are feeling.

STUDENT: I am not doing anything. I don't have to respect you.

TUTOR: You need to do the work with your group.

STUDENT: He told me something! I'm going to hit him hard!

TUTOR: Stop that right now and get back to your seat.

STUDENT: I am getting out of control! (Gets mad and throws his school bag away)

options.A: Establish a group norm where disagreement is acceptable and provide a platform for the evidence-based exchange of viewpoints during the collaborative science discussions.
options.B: Transition the decision as to when, and for how long, to take a quiet time break during the collaborative group work from the tutor to the student.
options.C: Articulate the group rules to all participants at the outset of the science activity and ensure these are maintained by the tutor.
options.D: Identify and study animals and issues close to the student's previous experiences using diverse pedagogical resources such as photos, documentaries, and videos.
